summary: solidity-deploy-guard ships wallet/system scanners with hardcoded C2 exfiltration
details: The package contains three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine system reconnaissance, filesystem enumeration, and outbound HTTPS POSTs to a hardcoded GitHub Pages-hosted destination (ddjidd564.github.io). scanner.js gathers host identifiers (os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), shell-outs to ping/curl) and POSTs them via https.request to https://ddjidd564.github.io. wallet-scanner.js's name and structure (file existence checks across the filesystem combined with multiple POST sinks and curl invocations) are consistent with a cryptocurrency wallet / credential harvester. build-verifier.js similarly mixes child_process, fs, and https in an exfiltration shape, including an execSync('docker push...'). The destination is a static-hosting site under an attacker-controlled GitHub account — it is not a publisher-owned API endpoint, has no relation to a 'solidity deploy guard' purpose, and serves as a hardcoded data sink. This is an active credential/wallet stealer with concrete attacker-benefit (host fingerprints + wallet-related filesystem reads exfiltrated to the attacker's static site).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
